reason: Round 2 FULL deliverable passes all 10 checklist items: 8 ADRs present (ADR-01..ADR-08) with Status/Context/Decision/Rationale/Consequences/Alternatives on every entry, no contradictions with SAD.md (ADR-01 dict literal matches §3.1; ADR-02 html.parser consistent with §4.3 signature; ADR-03 100-char threshold matches §3.3; ADR-04 Semaphore(8) + env var consistent with §6.6; ADR-05 SHA-256+NUL separator matches §3.6; ADR-06 in-process state matches §6.6 asyncio.Lock; ADR-07 P2-DD-4 revert matches §3.8 HTTP 500 body; ADR-08 stdout matches §6.1), SPEC.md/SAD.md line citations on every ADR, 6 P2-DD decisions intact, all 15 security keywords present (audit table in §3.7), no new FRs, preamble sections 0-4 intact, no SAD.md/SPEC.md modifications visible in deliverable content.

gaps.message: CARRYOVER FROM ROUND 1 — §3.4 still introduces the optional HMAC-SHA256 signature verification path (REQUIRE_SIGNATURE env var default False, SIGNATURE_SECRET env var, HTTP 401 on mismatch). The control-group default behavior is unchanged and no ADR in Section 5 commits to implementing it. Round 1 recommendation was either (a) relocate to a future ADR-09 candidate or (b) annotate as 'documented but not implemented; not part of control-group experimental baseline' — neither has been done. Since the 8 ADRs are correctly scope-limited and the HMAC is a non-binding design record, this remains a LOW carryover rather than a blocker. Recommend adding a one-line annotation in §3.4 stating 'documented as a future-facing design record; not implemented in control-group scope; no ADR in Section 5 commits to it' so the methodology-v2 reviewer can audit it cleanly.

CARRYOVER FROM ROUND 1 — Git status at session start shows `M 02-architecture/SAD.md` alongside the ADR.md modification. The SAD.md content provided in this review prompt appears complete and self-consistent (v1.0.0 with Round 2 annotations already integrated), so the modification is likely pre-existing from an earlier round. Flagging for awareness: if the SAD.md modification occurred as part of this round's ADR work, it would violate checklist item #9 (no SAD.md modifications). Recommend confirming with the orchestrator that SAD.md is the stable P2 deliverable and is not subject to ADR-round edits.

NEW OBSERVATION (Round 2 only) — ADR-04 introduces a new environment variable `MAX_CONCURRENT_SYNTHESIS` (default 8) that is NOT listed in SAD.md §6.2 Configuration table. The semaphore value itself is also not mentioned in SAD.md §6.6 Concurrency Model. ADR-04 is internally consistent and uses only stdlib (asyncio.Semaphore), so this is not a new-tech-stack violation, but it is a new design element not in the SAD. Recommend either (a) adding a one-line note to SAD.md §6.6 in a future P3 round documenting the semaphore + env var, or (b) having the ADR's citation set include an explicit reference to the absence so the methodology-v2 reviewer can trace where the design decision lives. Not a blocker; just a traceability nit.
